title: Public Records Request – Grass Mitigation and Ground Maintenance Contracts (Predecessor to RFP #350776)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request – Grass Mitigation and Ground Maintenance Contracts (Predecessor to RFP #350776)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request copies of the following records related to the City of Memphis's grass mitigation, lot cleaning, and ground maintenance program administered by the Division of Community Enhancement. This request is made in connection with RFP #350776 (Grass Mitigation and Ground Maintenance), currently open with a due date of April 15, 2026, and is intended to cover the predecessor contract(s) to that solicitation.
Specifically, I am requesting the following for the period January 1, 2021 through the present:

All awarded contracts, task orders, and amendments for grass mitigation, lot mowing/cutting, nuisance-lot abatement, and related ground maintenance services, including the name of each awarded contractor and the contract term.
The pricing schedules and unit rates contained in each of the above contracts.
Total amounts paid to each contractor under those contracts, broken out by fiscal year if possible. Payment registers, vendor ledgers, or purchase-order summaries identifying vendor name, invoice date, invoice amount, and service description would satisfy this item.
Work-volume records sufficient to show the quantity of service performed under those contracts — for example, number of parcels serviced, acres cut, and/or work orders issued, by month or by fiscal year.
The original solicitation documents (RFP, IFB, or RFQ) that resulted in the currently active and most recent prior grass mitigation / ground maintenance contracts, including any bid tabulations, scoring sheets, and notices of award.
Any internal estimates, forecasts, or staff memoranda prepared in the last 24 months projecting annual acreage, parcel counts, or budget for the grass mitigation and ground maintenance program.
The current inventory or count of vacant/nuisance parcels maintained by the Division of Community Enhancement that are eligible for mitigation services, if such a list or count exists.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions. If you anticipate fees in excess of $25, please contact me for authorization before proceeding.
I am requesting these records in electronic format (PDF, CSV, or Excel) delivered through the MuckRock portal. I am a Tennessee citizen (note: TPRA access is limited to Tennessee citizens — if you are not a TN resident, have a TN-based colleague file it, or MuckRock can help route it).
Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.

title: 2024 to Present AI Usage Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: 2024 to Present AI Usage Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All generative AI conversation logs — including user prompts/inputs and AI-generated outputs/responses — created by city employees using any of the following platforms: Microsoft Copilot ($45K budgeted FY 2024-2025), ChatGPT Enterprise, and any other generative AI chatbot tool. Time period: January 1, 2024 to present.

2. Administrative usage logs or reports showing the number of employees with access to, and usage statistics for, each generative AI platform deployed by the city during this period.

3. For ChatGPT Enterprise accounts: the data export available through the admin console (ZIP archive containing conversation.html files and metadata JSON). For Microsoft 365 Copilot: the conversation history logs available through the M365 admin center or Purview compliance portal.

This request encompasses all city employees and contractors with access to these tools. I am not requesting the tools' training data or source code — only the conversation logs between users and AI systems.

If any records are withheld, please cite the specific statutory exemption and release all segregable portions.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Raghav Sinha

communications.subject: Records Request Confirmation
communications.communication:
Dear Lia Jones, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a request for public information. This is your Request Number: 2026-102-MPIA and your security key is EB1B765A. Please retain this information, as it will be required when communicating with the City Clerk regarding your request. 
  
 Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, the City will provide an initial response within 10 working days of receipt of your request. If additional time is required to complete the request, you will be notified accordingly. If you did not provide contact information at the time of submission, you are responsible for contacting the City Clerk’s Office to confirm receipt and check the status of your request. In all communications or when picking up records, please reference your request number and security key. 
  
 Md. Code, Gen. Prov. Art., § 4-206 permits the City to charge reasonable fees for the search for and response to a Public Information Act request. The City charges a fee equivalent to the employee working on the request’s hourly pay times the number of hours worked. The first two hours are free. 
  
 If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact: Lia Jones, City Clerk, Phone: 240-805-1084, Email: cityclerk@gaithersburgmd.gov.  You may also use this system to upload any additional documents related to your request.

title: Alex Golesh Head Football Coach Employment Contract

communications.subject: Alabama Open Records Law Request: Alex Golesh Head Football Coach Employment Contract
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The complete, executed employment agreement, contract, and/or memorandum of understanding between Auburn University (and/or the Auburn University Board of Trustees) and Alex Golesh relating to his employment as head football coach, which was entered into on or about November 30, 2025.
Any and all amendments, addenda, side letters, term sheets, or supplemental agreements to that contract executed to date.

Any schedules, exhibits, or attachments referenced in or incorporated into the contract, including (but not limited to) those describing base salary, supplemental/outside marketing and promotional income, retention payments, performance bonuses, buyout/liquidated damages provisions, and benefits or perquisites.

I am requesting the complete, executed document(s), including all signature pages. If any portion is considered exempt from disclosure, please redact only that portion and release the remainder, along with a citation to the specific legal authority for each redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Truman Burrage.

communications.communication:

title: FOIA - Fox - US NAVY Aviator

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA - Fox - US NAVY Aviator
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) for Mark I. Fox
Request copy of history of assignments NAVPERS form 1070/605, Legion of Merit award citations, Meritorious Service medal citation, Bronze Star Medal citation, Navy Commendation medal citations, navy achievement medal citations, Air Medal award citations, all award citations, Cumulative Record of Training (NAVPERS Form 5300/16), Transfers and Receipts NAVPERS 601-12 from 1970 to 2000, Administrative remarks NAVPERS 1070/613, dates of promotions.
Please redact any personal information per Freedom of Information Act, U.S.C. § 552(b)(3) and U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).

VETERAN INFORMATION
Name of veteran: Mark I. Fox
Branch of service: United States Navy
Rank: VADM
Service: 1978 to 2016

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: NCAA Financial Statement

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: NCAA Financial Statement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern,

I request the NCAA Membership Financial Reporting System revenue and expense reports for fiscal (or reporting) years 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007 & 2008.  Specifically:  

These reports are completed by the athletic department for the National Collegiate Athletic Association for the fiscal years listed above. This report is a multi-page document that had to be submitted to the NCAA in January. It contains 30+ revenue and expense categories, followed by specific breakdowns of each of those categories, by sport and gender. I am requesting the full report, including the detail tables, the “Other Reporting Items” list and the “Statement of Revenues and Expenses” that appears at the end of the report. PLEASE NOTE: The NCAA report is different than the equity report that is sent to the U.S. Department of Education for Title IX compliance.

If your department does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian’s name and address.

I prefer to receive the records electronically as an Adobe Acrobat (.pdf) file.

If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) on which you rely.  Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Stacey Brook.

title: BWC & Incident Records: Deputy Harodis Nunez Shot in Chest During Family Disturbance – Davenport, FL (October 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the shooting of Deputy Harodis Nunez during a family disturbance call.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 2022
Location: Tanglewood Preserve subdivision, Davenport, Polk County, Florida
Suspect Name: Gabriel Batista, 41
Victim Officer: Deputy Harodis Nunez (shot in the chest, saved by bulletproof vest)
 
Incident Summary: Deputies responded to a family disturbance at approximately 10 p.m. While deputies were on scene, suspect Gabriel Batista returned home and drove past two marked patrol cars, confronting two deputies in the front yard. Batista kept his hands behind his back and refused to show them when deputies asked. He then shot Deputy Nunez once in the chest. The deputy's bulletproof vest saved his life. Deputies returned fire. Nunez was released from the hospital two days later. Sheriff Grady Judd released bodycam footage at a press conference.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Deputy Nunez and all other responding deputies involved in the disturbance call and shooting.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the initial disturbance call.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Fatal Shooting of Senior Airman Roger Fortson – Fort Walton Beach, FL (May 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal shooting of Senior Airman Roger Fortson by an Okaloosa County Sheriff's deputy.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: May 3, 2024
Location: Apartment 1401, apartment complex in Fort Walton Beach, Okaloosa County, Florida
Victim: Senior Airman Roger Fortson, 23 (deceased)
Arresting Agency: Okaloosa County Sheriff's Office
 
Incident Summary: A deputy responded to a reported disturbance at the apartment complex at approximately 4:28 p.m. A woman at the complex told the deputy she heard yelling and possibly a slap coming from apartment 1401. The deputy took the elevator to the fourth floor, knocked three times, and announced "Sheriff's office, open the door" twice. Within seconds of Fortson opening the door, the deputy said "Step back" and fired his weapon, fatally shooting Fortson. Fortson was holding a legally owned firearm at his side. He later died from his injuries. The victim's family, represented by attorney Ben Crump, disputed claims about the incident.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the shooting deputy and all other deputies who responded to the apartment complex.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident report, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings related to the initial disturbance report, including the call that prompted the deputy's response.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any statements taken from witnesses at the apartment complex.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic
